front: Work: the protagonist of this novel is born with a piece of jade in his mouth
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the goddess Nuwa rejects a stone when repairing the sky, and it becomes the jade in the protagonist's mouth in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: a priest and a monk carry the protagonist through the Land of Illusion to the fairy Disenchantment's palace in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: this novel opens with the couplet 'Truth becomes fiction when the fiction's true; Real becomes not-real where the unreal's real'
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the Crimson Pearl Flower is reborn alongside the stone; her tears are shed 'during the whole of a mortal lifetime' in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the protagonist is tricked into marrying Xue Baochai while thinking he was marrying Lin Daiyu in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the protagonist has his first sexual experience ('the Art of Love') with the maid Aroma after an erotic dream in the World of Illusion in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: at age one, the protagonist chose women's cosmetics from an array of career-symbolizing objects in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the protagonist's father wants him to study Confucian classics but he reads erotica and Zhuangzi instead in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the protagonist becomes lethargic when a begonia tree blooms inauspiciously out of season in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: one sickly character buries flower petals and writes an elegiac poem asking 'who will bury me when dead I lie' in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)
work: Lin Daiyu and the Flower Burial

front: Work: the protagonist's true love Lin Daiyu dies of grief after learning he was tricked into marrying another in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)
work: Lin Daiyu and the Flower Burial

front: Work: the 12 Beauties of Jinling appear as allegorical poems in a dream in Chapter 5 of this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: a poetry contest leads friends to write twelve poems about chrysanthemums in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: a major debate in 'Redology' concerns whether the last 40 chapters of this novel are authentic or later additions to the first 80
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: David Hawkes translated this novel, giving characters Latinate names like Sapientia and Adamantina
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the Cheng-Gao editions added 40 chapters with dubious authorial provenance to this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: manuscripts of this novel contain commentary by Zhiyanzhai ('Inkstone Studio')
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: much of this novel takes place in the Grand View / Prospect Garden
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)
work: Grand View / Prospect Garden and Household

front: Work: the Rongguo and Ningguo branches of the family build adjacent compounds in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: Wang Xifeng, whose name means 'Splendid Phoenix,' manages the household in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: a purse with an indecent picture causes all maids to come under suspicion of illicit affairs in this novel
back: Dream of the Red Chamber (Cao Xueqin)

front: Work: the family declines until the mansion is ransacked by imperial troops in this novel
